title: APUSH date-tagged developments — anachronism verifier + distractor pool
note: Each development is a real historical development with a year (or range), period, and theme tags. Two roles: (1) ANACHRONISM VERIFIER — the generator/checker confirms a keyed CAUSE predates the stimulus and a keyed EFFECT postdates it, and that a 'wrong_era' distractor genuinely violates the stimulus era; (2) DISTRACTOR POOL — period-plausible developments to draw true-but-irrelevant / wrong-era / scope-mismatch distractors from. Dates are standard textbook dates; verify against a public-domain reference (OpenStax US History) at generation time. This is a SEED set for core periods 3-8; extend via data/build_seed_corpus.py.
fields: id | name | year (int or [start,end]) | period | themes[] | one-line

developments.name: Columbian Exchange
developments.desc: transatlantic transfer of crops, animals, people, and disease

developments.name: Spanish encomienda system
developments.desc: forced-labor grants over Native peoples

developments.desc: first permanent English colony; joint-stock, tobacco economy

developments.desc: early self-government covenant in Plymouth

developments.name: Bacon's Rebellion
developments.desc: backcountry revolt; accelerated shift from indentured servitude to chattel slavery

developments.name: First Great Awakening
developments.desc: evangelical revival; challenged established churches

developments.desc: lax British enforcement fostered colonial self-rule

developments.desc: imperial war; British debt drove new colonial taxes

developments.desc: asserted Parliament's authority to bind colonies 'in all cases'
